text.title: EVP Agent Rules and Antipatterns
text.summary: Proof-point gate, common EVP antipatterns, and agent-specific failure modes including DEI and localization risks.

rule.statement: Reject any EVP pillar that cannot be backed by at least three named proof points from internal programs, budgets, or data. Replace unsupported claims with UNVERIFIED placeholders for human fill-in.
rule.rationale: Aspirational copy without evidence is detectable by candidates within the first 30 days and surfaces as Glassdoor reviews citing "company doesn't match the pitch." This destroys recruiter credibility.

antipattern.description: Generic claims: "innovative culture," "great people," "make a difference."
antipattern.reason: Every employer says this. Generic claims provide zero differentiation and signal that the EVP was written without employee research.

antipattern.description: One-size-fits-all messaging across all role families.
antipattern.reason: Engineers care primarily about technical growth; sales reps care about uncapped earnings; ops cares about process and stability. A single message under-converts all three.

antipattern.description: Never updating the EVP after initial launch.
antipattern.reason: EVP becomes stale as benefits, culture, and leadership change. An EVP that no longer matches reality is worse than no EVP — it creates disappointed hires who become Glassdoor detractors.

rule.statement: Never invent diversity statistics. Use UNVERIFIED placeholders that humans fill from HRIS. Published false diversity metrics are a serious reputation and legal risk.
rule.rationale: DEI claims without internal data are reputation risk; agents synthesizing from public sources will produce percentages that contradict what employees see daily.

rule.statement: Run a post-pass stripping generic clichés ("innovative," "passionate," "fast-paced," "world-class") from every EVP draft.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to these phrases; they add word count with zero signal and are the most common reason candidates call EVPs "corporate speak."

antipattern.description: Direct translation of a US-centric EVP to EU/LATAM markets without localization (e.g., equity framing in countries where options are taxed at grant).
antipattern.reason: Localization failures produce legally inaccurate or culturally tone-deaf offers that candidates reject at higher rates than generic ones.

text.reference: Competitor EVP comparison table and positioning map.
